description: Confounder: The model assumes that renormalization always yields values summing to route_sca

_meta.explanation: The rule 'When using ExpertChoiceRouter with per-token gate renormalization, the renormalized gate values for each token sum to route_scale, not 1, due to explicit scaling by route_scale after normalization.' does NOT hold here. The model assumes that renormalization always yields values summing to route_scale per token, as in the confirming case. However, when all gate values for a token are zero, token_score_sums becomes zero, and division by near-zero (with epsilon) yields finite but unnormalized values that do not reflect true proportions, breaking the sum-to-route_scale behavior.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'When using ExpertChoiceRouter with per-token gate renormalization, the renormalized gate values for each token sum to route_scale, not 1, due to explicit scaling by route_scale after normalization.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
